description:
Advances: specled.expanded_behavior_tier

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Realization.ExpandedBehavior reads :beam_lib.chunks(beam, [:debug_info]), extracts post-expansion AST per declared MFA, canonicalizes via SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical.normalize/1, and hashes deterministically (term_to_binary [:deterministic, minor_version: 2]); emits detector_unavailable: :debug_info_stripped on modules compiled with @compile {:no_debug_info, true}; SpecLedEx.Realization.Typespecs hashes sorted @spec/@type declarations per MFA/type; mixed-debug-info fixture expansion (test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex with @compile {:no_debug_info, true}); other sample_project modules retain default debug_info.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.expanded_hash_stable_on_rename
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.no_debug_info_degrades
  - specled.expanded_behavior_tier.scenario.typespec_arg_change_drifts

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/typespecs.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs
- test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex
- test/integration/ (mixed-debug-info integration test only)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex (S5b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (S5b extends, not here)
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT extend Compiler.Context schema or HashStore schema here; if expanded_behavior needs new fields, flag in PR (S5a and S3a are constrained to not run in parallel for this reason).
- Reuse SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical unchanged; do NOT fork an expanded-AST canonicalizer.
- Typespecs hashing sorts declarations lexicographically before hashing to prevent order-induced drift.
- Missing debug_info must degrade gracefully (detector_unavailable), never raise.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Mixed-debug-info fixture green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Tests added (23 new tests, all green):
  - SpecLedEx.Realization.ExpandedBehaviorTest (11 tests)
  - SpecLedEx.Realization.TypespecsTest (8 tests)
  - SpecLedEx.Integration.MixedDebugInfoFixtureTest (4 tests, @tag :integration)

Verification stubs flipped (4 stubs in expanded_behavior_tier.spec.md):
  - kind: command, target: mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs
  - kind: command, target: mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs
  - kind: source_file, target: test/fixtures/sample_project/lib/sample_project/no_debug.ex

Summary:
ExpandedBehavior.hash/2 pulls `:beam_lib.chunks(beam, [:debug_info])` and extracts the elixir_v1 `module_map` via `backend.debug_info/4`, then canonicalizes the matching MFA's clauses through the existing SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical (no fork, per Out-of-Scope intent) and hashes with the deterministic term_to_binary+SHA-256 pipeline. Typespecs.hash/2 pulls per-MFA `@spec` declarations via Code.Typespec.fetch_specs/1 and module-level `@type` declarations via Code.Typespec.fetch_types/1, strips typespec-form line metadata recursively, and sorts declarations on their serialized form before hashing so source-order swaps cannot induce drift. Both tiers emit detector_unavailable (reason: debug_info_stripped) once per module on the stripped path — collapsing duplicate bindings — and never raise. Modern Elixir only strips Elixir debug_info via `@compile {:debug_info, false}`, not `{:no_debug_info, true}` (the Erlang-side Abst-chunk option); the fixture sets both so the beam is fully stripped across supported versions. `mix spec.check` shows 0 findings on specled.expanded_behavior_tier; the 3 remaining warnings are pre-existing specled.use_tier baseline (S5b / q59.8 scope). Compiler.Context schema and HashStore schema unchanged per Out-of-Scope intent.

VERIFY: PASS

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
  * mix test test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs -> 11 tests, 0 failures
  * mix test test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs -> 8 tests, 0 failures

- Requirements with passing tests: 6/6
  * reads_beam_debug_info -> test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs:154 "returns {:ok, binary} for a function that lives only in expansion" (hashes DSL-emitted handle_event/2 which has no source defn)
  * hash_stable_on_refactor -> test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs:181 "hash is stable when the DSL-emitted body is only cosmetically renamed" (also :217 confirms real semantic change flips)
  * no_debug_info_detector_unavailable -> test/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior_test.exs:253 (hash/2 returns {:error, {:debug_info_stripped, _}}) and :286 (run/3 emits detector_unavailable, no drift)
  * typespecs_hashes_spec_and_type -> test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs:118 (hash produces 32-byte binary) and :152 (reorder-invariant)
  * typespecs_drift_finding -> test/specled_ex/realization/typespecs_test.exs:178 (hash flips on integer()->binary()) and :215 (run/3 emits branch_guard_realization_drift tier=typespecs); also :132 whitespace-only reformat does NOT flip
  * mixed_fixture_coverage -> test/integration/mixed_debug_info_fixture_test.exs:56/63/69/75 (Sample hashes, SampleProject.NoDebug degrades on both tiers, collapse-to-one finding)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  * No Compiler.Context schema diff (git diff b5c0278^..b5c0278 -- lib/specled_ex/compiler/ is empty)
  * No HashStore schema diff (empty diff for lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex)
  * ExpandedBehavior reuses Canonical (no fork): expanded_behavior.ex:67 -> Canonical.hash(Canonical.normalize(ast))
  * Typespecs sorts declarations lexicographically: typespecs.ex:238-242 canonical_list/1 uses Enum.sort_by(&:erlang.term_to_binary(&1, [:deterministic, minor_version: 2]))
  * Neither tier raises on stripped path: zero `raise` occurrences in expanded_behavior.ex or typespecs.ex; both hash/2 and run/3 consistently return tagged tuples / finding maps; beam_lib errors routed to {:error, {:debug_info_stripped, mod}}
  * Deterministic hashing ADR honored: canonical.ex:62 and typespecs.ex:241 both pass [:deterministic, minor_version: 2]
- Project verification (mix spec.check): exits non-zero with 3 warnings, ALL attributed to specled.use_tier (lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex + two use_tier test files). These are S5b / q59.8 scope — pre-existing baseline left intact by this ticket. 0 findings attributed to specled.expanded_behavior_tier.
- Full test suite: 373 tests, 0 failures (mix test --include integration)

Dual-attribute fixture evaluation: CONFIRMED — the implementer's claim is factually correct on Elixir 1.18/OTP 27. Reproduced via elixirc of four single-attribute vs dual-attribute modules and :beam_lib.chunks(bin, [:debug_info]):
  * @compile {:no_debug_info, true} alone -> debug_info chunk PRESENT ({:debug_info_v1, :elixir_erl, <data>}) // NOT stripped
  * @compile {:debug_info, false} alone -> STRIPPED ({:debug_info_v1, :elixir_erl, :none})
  * both attributes -> STRIPPED (matches fixture)
  * default -> PRESENT
The fixture uses both so it honors the spec's literal wording (@compile {:no_debug_info, true}) while actually producing a debug_info-less beam. This is a pragmatic correctness choice, not a spec-weakening change.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.resolve/2 beam-first rule from specled.decision.beam_first_binding_resolution is not directly invoked by ExpandedBehavior.hash/2; instead the tier does its own Code.ensure_loaded -> :beam_lib.chunks -> backend.debug_info(:elixir_v1, ...) walk. This is consistent with the decision's scope (resolve/2 is the module-level source/beam resolver for arbitrary MFA text, whereas expanded_behavior goes straight to debug_info by design). No concern, but flagging for auditor awareness.
- Unit tests rely on Kernel.ParallelCompiler.compile_to_path/2 after forcing Code.put_compiler_option(:debug_info, true) because ExUnit runs can leave the global compiler option flipped. Reasonable workaround; documented inline.
- "warning: redefining module ..." noise in ExpandedBehaviorTest + TypespecsTest is expected (fixtures recompile the same module names across scenarios) and does not reflect a defect.
- Umbrella degrade path exercised via ExpandedBehaviorTest run/3 umbrella graceful degrade and mirrored in Typespecs — consistent with specled.decision.umbrella_deferred_v1_1.
